changelog: Guardrail nudge tier — a middle outcome between hard-block and confirm for destructive-but-sometimes-necessary commands (the rm -rf class). On a plain match the hook now DENIES (exit 2) with a 'try a safer alternative first' message (mv aside / git rm / trash / dry-run / tighter scope) AND tells the agent exactly how to escalate; if the agent has confirmed there is no reversible alternative, it re-runs the SAME command with a `# guardrail:confirm` comment marker, which flips the decision to a user confirm (ask). Stateless + auditable: the escalation marker travels in the re-issued command, so it can't be applied silently and the user sees it in what they approve. Precedence: dangerousPatterns/block_patterns (hard, never escalatable, ignore the marker) -> nudge_patterns (deny->confirm-on-marker) -> confirm_patterns (straight ask) -> pass. New nudge_patterns YAML tier (rm -rf / del /s /q / Remove-Item -Recurse -Force moved here from confirm_patterns, temp-dir first-arg exemption preserved). _lib/guardrail-rules.js gains nudge handling + hasEscalationMarker/ESCALATION_MARKER; _lib/command-blocker.js gains the formatNudge message; guardrail.cjs routes nudge via the block channel. Live-yaml + fixture + unit tests updated/added (1876 passing). Motivated by user ask: 'when we hit a guardrail like rm -rf, try something else first, and only confirm if there's no other way.'
